reviews.review_msg: This was my third class with Antone and I was once again NOT disappointed. His lectures and discussions are very informative and engaging. You can tell he really enjoys what he is teaching. He gives great feedback and is a very fair marker. Super caring prof, definitely recommend!

Ronda made Shakespeare enjoyable. She picked a great selection of plays and focused on the texts themselves, rather than critical theory. This lecture-style made the task of talking about Shakespeare less daunting. Her papers were typical of the third year level. She gives you a choice on what to write on. Not a difficult marker, just picky.

This was one of my favourite classes I ever took. Ronda was such a good lecturer and fantastic at creating student discussion. She came well prepared with engaging lecture content and was always very clear in her expectations. She answers emails fast and encouraged people to come to office hours, where she gave very good feedback on papers.

She would sometimes have scattered ideas and be all over the place during lectures sometimes, but would be helpful a lot of the time. A lot of lectures don't focus on works themselves, but around other things like movie adaptations which added enjoyment. However, they weren't really helpful for the course.
Nice, but boring and unfocused.

Dr. G is an excellent professor. Show him respect and join in the discussion. He wants students to learn and have ideas, not just sit there say nothing. He marks papers fairly and offers really good feedback. His knowledge of and enthusiasm for Shakespeare is refreshing. He has great energy, so go on take the class!

He's passionate and extremely knowledgeable about Shakespeare, and as far as facilitating class discussion, he does that well. He's a tough marker though (do NOT take his term paper proposals lightly - although they're not worth much, he takes them more seriously than you would think). Overall nice guy, but I wish he would go easier on the marking.

This is my last semester and 4th time taking a class with Torsten. I cannot stress enough that if there is an opportunity to take a class with him, you should take it. Without a doubt my favourite prof at SFU. He is kind, helpful, and understands that students lives are busy. He is very accommodating and passionate about what he teaches.

The fact that this is the third class I've taken with him says something about how much I enjoy his class. Make sure to write in your books as much as you can, as all the exams I've taken with him have been open book with notes allowed as long as they're written in the book (you can use post-its too). Marks papers a little on the tougher side.

Tied with Everton for best English prof at SFU. Has the ability to make a four-hour night class relatively entertaining which is a definite plus. Fair grader, great sense of humour... wouldn't hestitate to take another course with him.

Yes, Paul Budra has a cocky air about him, but he's surprisingly modest and kind to ignorant arrogant remarks from students; in fact, he seems to encourage them. His lecture style is clearly engaging and fun, but he goes too quickly for the complex, more important things, and not quickly enough for the less. Still, one of the best at SFU.
I enjoyed Paul's class more than any other at SFU. He has an awsome sense of humour, a vast knowledge of material and is extremely approachable. If at all possible take a class with him!!!

has got to be the best prof in the engl dept. and no, it's not cause i think he's 'hot'. he made shakespeare fun and his classes were NEVER boring. lectures are 9:30 in the morn & taped, but i always went cause it was just so entertaining as well as intellectually stimilating ofcourse. he's also very helpful w/ emails and concerns. he sais ew alot.
No Comments
A great professor!  He made Shakespeare fun and interesting.
Great prof, i heard he used to be a stand up comic! Need i say more
Budra rocks! Amazing lecturer, great tutorials, funny, and sexy too!
Such a great professor I've enrolled in his class 3 times...great class to take to gain credits with ease..he made my 4 course load feel like 3.

One of the most easy-going, helpful, engaging profs I've ever had. He knows his stuff, and he actually cares that his students learn it, too. He is beyond reasonable, understanding, and witty. Anyone who says otherwise is just afraid to be out-classed. Attend just ONE day--just the first class, please, and judge for yourself.

Although he is constantly joking and thinks he is hilarious, he isn't. His three hour lectures were monotonous and full of rambling. He was also very disorganized.However, the lectures often had really good points to consider, and papers were very relevant with laid-back guidelines. Not too bad, but probably wouldn't take another with Torsten.
Just a really nice guy
Excellent prof!!! Very nice, very funny (at least I think so), and shows that he is passionate about what he's teaching. Definitely recommend him, one of the best at SFU.

Awesome. Makes learning this subject interesting and fun. This is the way teachers were meant to teach. His lectures are funny and he managed to make a boring topic really interesting... Best Proff to have... I would recommend all of his classes whether they are interesting or not...

Torsten was a great prof, he is very intellignet and knows his Shakespeare.  He made class interesting and you were never bored.  Reading Shakespeare was made easier and alot more fun.  Highly recommended that people take his classes.  He never gave out too much work.
He was a really funny prof and a good teacher, but id have to say i hate early shakespeare...talk about dull and boring!  No offence Thorsten..you were a great prof!

Kind, funny, engaging prof. Class is self guided, especially since sometimes Torston seems scattered and unable to be critically analytical about material. Sometimes his lectures can come across as plot summary rather than helpful lit discussions. So unorganized that it hinders student's abilities

torsten is the nicest guy ever.  EVER.  he's really engaging, i always listen in class and he's hilarious too.  totally understanding with extensions and stuff.  but don't take advantage of him, he's too good a person for that!  :)
Torsten is an awesome prof. Very understanding in assignments. Very approachable. A chocolate addict who loves his Shakespeare. If you can't decide which course to take, take his!

I'm never intimidated in Torsten's classes. I was never one to participate that much in seminars but in his classes he puts you at ease and responds well to even dumb questions! Makes you feel like you know stuff and always motivates students.
Torsten is a great prof.  Sometimes goes off on very hilarious tangents.  The most understanding prof I've had when it comes to assignments.
